skill.name: lark-vc
skill.description: Lark Video Conference: Query meeting records, retrieve meeting notes including summaries, action items, chapters, and transcripts
skill.summary: Query Lark video conference records and retrieve AI-generated meeting notes including summaries, action items, chapters, and verbatim transcripts
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 209 patterns but all are false positives. The files are documentation markdown showing CLI command examples, not executable code. Path traversal patterns are relative documentation links (../lark-shared/SKILL.md). External command patterns are code examples in fenced blocks. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Query Lark meeting records and notes
content.value_statement: Need to find past meetings and retrieve AI-generated summaries? This skill searches Lark video conference records and extracts meeting notes, transcripts, and action items instantly.
actual_capabilities: Search historical meeting records by keyword, time range, organizer, participants, or room
Query meeting notes using meeting IDs, minute tokens, or calendar event IDs
Retrieve AI-generated summaries, action items, and chapter highlights
Download verbatim transcripts with speaker labels and timestamps
Access shared documents created during meetings
Support batch queries up to 50 meeting IDs per request
limitations: Only searches completed meetings, not future scheduled events
Requires user authentication with appropriate Lark OAuth scopes
Users must be meeting participants or have explicit access permissions
Maximum batch size of 50 IDs per query requires pagination for larger sets

use_cases.title: Project Manager Review
use_cases.description: Quickly retrieve summaries and action items from weekly team meetings to track project progress and assign follow-ups

use_cases.title: Absent Meeting Participant
use_cases.description: Catch up on meetings you couldn't attend by accessing AI summaries and full transcripts with speaker identification

use_cases.title: Meeting Documentation
use_cases.description: Export meeting records and transcripts for compliance documentation or knowledge base archival

prompt_templates.title: Basic Meeting Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for meetings I attended yesterday that contain the keyword 'sprint planning' and show me the results
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding recent meetings by keyword and timeframe

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve Meeting Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the meeting notes and AI summary for meeting ID 69xxxxxxxxxxxxx28, including any action items
prompt_templates.scenario: Extracting structured notes from a specific meeting

prompt_templates.title: Transcript Download
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the verbatim transcript for minute token obbxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx to the artifact directory

prompt_templates.title: Combined Meeting Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all meetings from last week organized by user ou_a in room 123, then retrieve the notes for each meeting that has a summary available
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex multi-step workflow combining search and notes retrieval

output_examples.input: Search for meetings with keyword 'weekly' from March 10-17, 2026
output_examples.output: Found 3 meetings: (1) Weekly Team Sync on Mar 12, 2h 15m, 8 participants; (2) Weekly Design Review on Mar 14, 1h, 5 participants; (3) Weekly Engineering Standup on Mar 16, 30m, 12 participants

Meeting: Q1 Planning Review. Summary: Discussed Q1 goals, identified 3 key initiatives, assigned ownership. Action Items: (1) Alice to finalize budget by Mar 20; (2) Bob to schedule customer interviews; (3) Carol to prepare demo. Documents: Smart notes link, Verbatim transcript link

best_practices: Always include at least one filter when searching (keyword, time range, or participant ID) to avoid overly broad results
Use pagination with page-token when total results exceed 50 meetings
Specify output-dir when downloading transcripts to keep artifacts organized
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to search future meetings; use lark-calendar skill for upcoming events
Do not guess API parameters; always run schema command first to view required structure
Do not assume batch queries return all results; always check has_more and page_token for pagination

faq.question: Can this skill search for future scheduled meetings?
faq.answer: No, lark-vc only searches completed historical meetings. Use the lark-calendar skill to query future scheduled events.

faq.question: What if I'm not a participant in the meeting I'm querying?
faq.answer: You need explicit access permissions. The API will return a permission error if you lack the required scope or are not a meeting participant.

faq.question: How do I get the meeting_id to query notes?
faq.answer: Use the +search command first to find meetings, then extract the meeting_id from the search results to pass to +notes.

faq.question: What is the difference between note_doc_token and verbatim_doc_token?
faq.answer: note_doc_token contains AI-generated summaries and action items. verbatim_doc_token contains the complete word-for-word transcript with speaker labels and timestamps.

faq.question: Can I query multiple meetings at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pass up to 50 meeting IDs or minute tokens in a single batch query, separated by commas.

faq.question: What authentication is required?
faq.answer: User OAuth authentication is required. Run 'lark-cli auth login' with the appropriate scopes for your query type before using this skill.
